@graph.schema:legalName: NOAA NMFS SEFSC Abundance and distribution surveys of cetaceans in U.S. Atlantic waters off the  Southeast U.S.
@graph.schema:name: NOAA NMFS SEFSC Abundance and distribution surveys of cetaceans in U.S. Atlantic waters off the  Southeast U.S.
@graph.schema:description: To meet the abundance and distribution data requirements of the Marine Mammal Protection Act, the Southeast Fisheries Science Centers conducts periodic aerial- and ship-based line-transect surveys of cetacean species that inhabit U.S waters off the Southeast U.S. Atlantic. Survey areas vary but are usually defined by U.S. waters south of the Virginia-Maryland border to Key West, Florida. Aerial surveys generally cover waters from the shoreline to continental shelf break (200-m isobath) or the mid-continental shelf depending on location. Ship surveys usually cover waters from the 50-m isobaths to the U.S. Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) boundary. Surveys were initiated in 1992 and are ongoing under the Atlantic Marine Assessment Program for Protected Species (AMAPPS). Ship surveys are usually conducted during the warm months of the year with a smaller number of cool/cold months surveys. Aerial surveys are uniformly distributed by seasons.

schema:geo.schema:description: Bounding box polygon with lat long (Y X) coordinate order.
